hypothesis: Applying an inverse-frequency class-balanced loss during training of the template-free RootAligned model will reduce the top-10 accuracy gap between rare (1–10 occurrences) and frequent (>10,000 occurrences) templates on USPTO-retro by at least 20 percentage points and increase Pistachio OOD top-10 accuracy by at least 1.0 percentage point, while not degrading USPTO-retro top-5 round-trip accuracy by more than 2 percentage points.
